prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response:
# The Octopus Who Hid in Plain Sight

Deep down at the bottom of the Wobbling Blue Sea, where the coral grew in castles and the fish wore stripes like pajamas, there lived an octopus named Oliver. Oliver had eight curling arms, two enormous golden eyes, and the very special talent of turning *exactly* the color of whatever he was hiding behind. And Oliver, more than almost anything, loved to hide. He hid behind purple sea fans. He hid beneath sandy rocks. He pressed himself flat against the ocean floor and disappeared like a whisper. The trouble was, Oliver didn't hide because he was playing a game. He hid because every time a friendly fish swam close and said hello, his cheeks flushed pink, his spots went polka-dotty, and all eight of his arms tied themselves into nervous little knots. More than anything in the whole wide ocean, Oliver wanted a friend. He just had absolutely no idea how to get one.
